title: [v5.2] Bump Buildah to v1.37.0
body:
The final step of the SIC vendor dance.  This bumps the following as noted:

c/storage v1.55.0
c/image v5.32.0
c/common v0.60
c/buildah v1.37.0

This is in preparation of Podman v5.2
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v5.2/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@edsantiago advice please!  These two tests are failing:  
Failed tests (2):
 - 124 bud-preserve-subvolumes
 - 136 bud-volume-perms
 
and they were included a couple weeks ago in Buildah via this PR: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5604.  In particular, this commit in the PR tweaked the two tests that are failing:   https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5604/commits/a7098c776a7fda585358c91496d35ce690ea9fb7

I thought they would work given the vendoring, but perhaps I'm missing a salient point?  I'll go follow the skip protocol, holler if that's the wrong route.
I've added a hold until @edsantiago can review and validate the test changes that I've made.
Also overall I strongly suggest that we merge into main first then cherry-pick, doing two PRs at the same time is confusing and doubles the reviewers work.
Is this one good to merge now?

No, this is not ready to merge. (1) Shouldn't this be done on main first, per our guidelines and as @Luap99 mentioned above? (2) the new `compat-*` options have not been wired into podman. They are documented, but nonfunctional (tests are skipped), and that seems suboptimal from a UX POV. [EDIT: (3) there's a weird whitespace addition in the new diffs]
Closing.  #23440 is the new attempt for the main branch.  If that goes, we do the vendor dance and then cherry-pick the second commit from there into this release-branch.
